item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd go mad to befriend Mr. Mad. The last line in the test with SCP-1212 ensures my upvote for this skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first mister that I can say I really liked, not as an SCP but as a person, he is so likable and genuinely nice! It's refreshing. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mad's final question pushes this into solid gold.

item.title: Re: Translating "Mad"
item.content:encoded: The double-meaning is pretty important, so it might be better to try to translate that than exact meaning. I know that hot-headed and hot-blooded translate well; is there a form that could also be read as "literally having a fever so high that you start hallucinating"?

item.title: Translating "Mad"
item.content:encoded:
The article kind of relies on Mad meaning both crazy and/or angry. Both concepts are used. Could you write it as Mr. Crazy/Angry with a footnote explaining that English has one word that means both? So translate:

Mr. Crazy/Angry1

Footnotes
1. Name is written in English, which has one word meaning both concepts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Translator from the Korean branch. What does the word "mad" in "Mr. Mad" stand for: "crazy" or "angry"? How can I say about the word in here and in the other tale?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder how he'd interact with the real creatures that can only be seen by schizophrenics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably my favorite Little Mister, and definitely in my top favorite SCPs. I do hope the doctors have more tests for him, I crave more to read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, when does this get turned into a collaborative? It seems ready-made for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
interesting. By the way, +1, for good writing and characterization.

And, it's an imaginary friend with an existential crisis, and that's hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it's not the entire document that causes SCP-2428's hallucination, it's the piece of paper that it's referring to. Whether Fred could be introduced to the actual anomaly, um. Hmm. Provided the Little Misters list would even qualify as something Fred could jump into, I guess he… hm. I guess you could argue it either way, he'd either just insert his name somewhere onto it or he'd also piggyback along with Mad into the hallucination.

TL;DR: It's an anomaly! Whatever sounds fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I'm just curious about how SCP-423 would interact with the document, and whether or not it would also be a hallucination, because if the document for SCP-2428 causes the hallucination, and 423 inserted himself into the document, would that cause him to be inserted into the hallucination itself, or just into the document and nothing else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And I laughed so hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilers.
- Spoilers.

So what 1212 does is make you engage in a drinking contest until you die through so much alcohol consumption. Because 1212 is a hallucination and Mr. Mad is a hallucination, what he is doing is intervening and engaging in a bar fight of sorts to prevent the D-Class from dying in the test.

- Spoilers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this. A well-done SCP that doesn't kill you or drive you insane. I'm not quite sure what happened in the cross-testing log, though. Can someone explain?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was really good and I especially liked the tie-in to 1212. But I came here to say that I really like the format of the test logs. They look so nice and neat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess what bothers me about it is that the test still feels like it was done "just for the sake of it".

While it did end up providing useful insight, it feels like there was no prior indication that that would be the case. The entry gives the feeling that the only reason for the test was "something interesting might happen, who knows", and that doesn't feel like a strong enough reason to kill even a class D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep in mind that at least a subset of the D-class are death row inmates of the "I ate his liver with some fava beans, and a nice Chianti." sort, and the population may or may not also include mass produced clones. D-class aren't cheap, but they are still a grey area as far as the ethics committee is concerned. And it did provide useful insight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, and I really like the execution, but the fourth test log ruins it for me. Did the foundation really straight up murder a class D just to see what would happen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well…
Obviously Misters are no longer the ordinary men with special power/changes on their body.

In the beginning, Soap is a man who creats soap; Hunger is a hungery man; Forgetful is a forgetful man, that sort of thing.

But, "Soap" can be many things. Like, a soap for…existence? A good skip is never the literial one.

It's fun to see a old series being added with new creative ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall I think this was quite good. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to know if they have any more tests for him. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, if Mr. Mad did what I think he did in that last test log, then I'm not surprised a fight broke out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels different from the conventional Little Misters, and that makes this article stand out among his brethren.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck yes!

It is also capable of perceiving and interacting with other hallucinations the subject experiences, regardless of whether they are anomalous.

At this point I knew it was going to be interesting. I was not disappointed.

+1 Fuckin' rad, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is much better than when I first saw it, and it was pretty good the first time around. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was a little iffy on this concept when I first heard it, but I think you pulled it off. The writing and structure are good. I particularly enjoyed the vent test, and the tests in general are good; Mr. Mad's character comes through in a compelling way.

+1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw yeah, more Little Misters! :D If there's any reason I'm upvoting this, it's hallucinatory drugs. Also, Mad is such an earnest, helpful kind of guy, it's hard not to like him. I love the test with the vent gremlin to death. I'm not as keen on the crosstest, but I think it came off well enough. If there's anything I don't like about this article, it's that weird table format for the test logs, but that's not enough to cancel my vote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We all go a little mad sometimes…

So the little misters tend to be pretty hit and miss with me. This one easily is my favorite of the existing misters so far, however, and there are two main reasons for that.

First off, I really dig the implication of all the little misters being normal people before they became the misters. That puts a really human face on a series that was all pretty human to begin with.

Second, I really like the interaction Mad has with the auditory hallucinations. That was my favorite part of the article, so much so that I could easily read an entire supplement log of similar interactions.

+1 TG.

Glad you got this posted.

item.title: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
Oh right I should make this post.

A thank you to Conwell, kaktus, Decibelle, and Kalinin for looking this over at various stages. A massive thank you to TyGently for going on with me about philosophy and such, and to thedeadlymoose for taking time out of the busy schedule to help me make this not stupid.

Hopefully we'll get a few more Misters in the nearish future.

Edit: eeee look at this hype art by SunnyClockwork!
